items.title: Open-weight models now dominate real-world AI usage, Hugging Face data shows
items.summary: TechCrunch reports Chinese open-weight models made up 41% of Hugging Face downloads this spring, surpassing US models, and on OpenRouter the six most-used models are all open-weight (Anthropic's Claude trails at #7). Nearly a third of requests on Vercel's platform in June 2026 ran on open-weight models, and half of Fortune 500 companies now deploy private or open-source models via Hugging Face.
items.whyItMatters: Reinforces that cheap open-weight models (in the vein of the DeepSeek fallback already wired into the tutor) are increasingly the default for non-frontier tasks like dictionary lookups and grammar generation — worth periodically re-checking which open model is cheapest/best for those calls rather than defaulting to a closed frontier model.

items.title: Vint Cerf backs DNSid, a DNS-based identity standard for autonomous AI agents
items.summary: Internet pioneer Vint Cerf has joined Innovation Labs (a subsidiary of Identity Digital) to help design DNSid, a proposed open standard that gives AI agents cryptographically verifiable identities tied to existing domain names, aiming to establish accountability as agents act more autonomously across the internet.
items.whyItMatters: As tutor agents like Romain and Anna take on more autonomous tool/API calls, an emerging standard for how agents identify and authenticate themselves to other services is worth tracking, even if adoption is still early.
